title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: evaluate whether variance-weighting improves clustering quality, need independent ground truth — one derived RNA clusters . use cbmc CITE-seq dataset SeuratData, measured RNA surface protein (ADT) 8,617 cells. ADT-based cell type labels (protein_annotations) assigned gating canonical surface markers (CD4, CD8, CD14, CD16, CD19, CD56, CD34) — measurement entirely independent RNA clustering evaluating. Two metrics computed method:

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) library(wUMAP) library(mclust)   # adjustedRandIndex library(RANN)     # fast k-NN data(cbmc) cbmc <- UpdateSeuratObject(cbmc)  # Keep only cells with protein labels; remove doublets cbmc <- cbmc[, !cbmc$protein_annotations %in% "T/Mono doublets" &                !is.na(cbmc$protein_annotations)]  cbmc <- NormalizeData(cbmc, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindVariableFeatures(cbmc, nfeatures = 2000, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- ScaleData(cbmc, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunPCA(cbmc, npcs = 30, verbose = FALSE) knn_purity <- function(emb, labels, k = 20) {   nn  <- RANN::nn2(emb, k = k + 1)$nn.idx[, -1]   lab <- as.integer(factor(labels))   mean(sapply(seq_len(nrow(nn)), function(i) mean(lab[nn[i, ]] == lab[i]))) }

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: set.seed(42) cbmc <- FindNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, resolution = 0.8, verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, reduction.name = "umap.std", verbose = FALSE)  ari_std <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_std <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.std"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
code: set.seed(42)  # stdev weighting cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                              prefix = "wt.sd", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.sd_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         reduction.name = "umap.sd", verbose = FALSE) ari_sd <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_sd <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.sd"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # stdev + log.scale cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                              log.scale = TRUE, prefix = "wt.sd.log",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.sd.log_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.sd.log",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_sd_log <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_sd_log <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.sd.log"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var, weight.factor = 0.5 cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              weight.factor = 0.5, prefix = "wt.pv05",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv05_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.5, reduction.name = "umap.pv05",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_pv05 <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv05 <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv05"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var, weight.factor = 1 cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              prefix = "wt.pv", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "umap.pv", verbose = FALSE) ari_pv <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # prop.var + log.scale cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                              log.scale = TRUE, prefix = "wt.pv.log",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.pv.log_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.pv.log",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_pv_log <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_pv_log <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.pv.log"), cbmc$protein_annotations)  # stdev + Marchenko-Pastur filtering: zero out noise PCs, weight signal PCs by stdev cbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                              mp.filter = TRUE, prefix = "wt.mp",                              verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "wt.mp_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev",                         mp.filter = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.mp",                         verbose = FALSE) ari_mp <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_mp <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.mp"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: RunLocalPCANeighbors() builds KNN/SNN graphs per-neighbourhood PCA distances stores consistent clustering visualisation. Using k.param local.weight.graph UMAP step ensures clustering layout share identical distances.
code: set.seed(42)  # Weighted local PCA: build KNN/SNN graphs with stdev-weighted local distances, # then cluster and visualise from the same graph. cbmc <- RunLocalPCANeighbors(cbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                              prefix = "lp", verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- FindClusters(cbmc, graph.name = "lp_snn", resolution = 0.8,                      verbose = FALSE) cbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(cbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "umap.lp", verbose = FALSE) ari_lp <- adjustedRandIndex(cbmc$seurat_clusters, cbmc$protein_annotations) pur_lp <- knn_purity(Embeddings(cbmc, "umap.lp"), cbmc$protein_annotations)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: Clustering quality vs protein labels cbmc CITE-seq (n ≈ 7,699 cells). ARI k-NN purity method. Dashed line = standard UMAP baseline. ARI shown local PCA UMAP (embedding-method).
code: results <- data.frame(   Method = c(     "standard (none)",     "stdev",     "stdev + log.scale",     "prop.var  wf=0.5",     "prop.var  wf=1",     "prop.var + log.scale",     "stdev + mp.filter",     "local PCA UMAP (stdev)"   ),   ARI       = c(ari_std, ari_sd, ari_sd_log, ari_pv05, ari_pv, ari_pv_log,                 ari_mp, ari_lp),   kNN_purity = c(pur_std, pur_sd, pur_sd_log, pur_pv05, pur_pv, pur_pv_log,                  pur_mp, pur_lp) ) knitr::kable(results, digits = 3,   caption = "Clustering quality vs protein labels on cbmc CITE-seq (n ≈ 7,699 cells).") library(tidyr)  res_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(results, cols = c("ARI", "kNN_purity"),                                 names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Score") res_long$Method <- factor(res_long$Method, levels = results$Method)  baseline <- data.frame(   Metric = c("ARI", "kNN_purity"),   Score  = c(ari_std, pur_std) )  ggplot(res_long[!is.na(res_long$Score), ],        aes(x = Method, y = Score, fill = Method)) +   geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_hline(data = baseline, aes(yintercept = Score),              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +   facet_wrap(~Metric, scales = "free_y") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   coord_flip() +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,        title = "wUMAP benchmark on cbmc CITE-seq",        subtitle = "Dashed line = standard UMAP baseline") +   theme_bw(base_size = 12)

what: UMAP layouts coloured by protein label
title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: methods coloured ADT-derived protein label.
code: Idents(cbmc) <- "protein_annotations"  plots <- list(   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.std",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("standard  ARI=%.3f", ari_std))    + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.sd",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("stdev  ARI=%.3f", ari_sd))        + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.sd.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("stdev+log  ARI=%.3f", ari_sd_log))+ NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv05",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var wf=0.5  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv05)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var wf=1  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.pv.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("prop.var+log  ARI=%.3f", ari_pv_log)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.mp",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("stdev+mp.filter  ARI=%.3f", ari_mp)) + NoLegend(),   DimPlot(cbmc, reduction = "umap.lp",    label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +     ggtitle(sprintf("local PCA (stdev)  ARI=%.3f", ari_lp)) + NoLegend() )  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2)

title: Benchmarking wUMAP Against Standard UMAP
text: PBMC-like dataset, aggressive prop.var weighting (wf=1) reduces clustering quality (ARI 0.548 → 0.318). PC 1 scRNA-seq captures monocyte–lymphocyte axis strongly emphasising collapses finer lymphocyte subtypes together. Key takeaways: stdev safest default — marginal ARI change, k-NN purity standard. prop.var weight.factor = 0.5 (partial blend) avoids worst -emphasising PC 1. log.scale = TRUE partially mitigates PC 1 dominance recovers ARI compared full prop.var. mp.filter = TRUE (combined stdev) automatically zeros noise PCs whose variance Marchenko–Pastur ceiling, applies chosen weighting remaining signal PCs. datasets PCs genuine signal behaves like base scheme; noisier datasets can sharpen clusters discarding uninformative PCs entirely. Local PCA (stdev) uses per-neighbourhood SVD distances weighted local standard deviation, RunLocalPCANeighbors() providing consistent clustering UMAP graphs. now fully integrated workflow comparable global weighting methods. k-NN purity differences generally small across methods, indicating UMAP topology broadly preserved — main effect Louvain cluster resolution. Recommendation: start default weight.= "stdev", use mp.filter = TRUE want principled, data-driven noise threshold. Use RunLocalPCANeighbors() + RunLocalPCAUMAP() suspect dataset strong local anisotropic structure (trajectories, gradients) global metric miss.

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: Standard UMAP treats every principal component (PC) equally. scRNA-seq, early PCs capture far biological variation later ones — yet standard UMAP PCs 1–50 weights PC 50 PC 1. wUMAP scales PC axis weight derived variance contribution handing embedding UMAP, biologically informative PCs dominate cell distances final layout. recommended default scheme weight.= "stdev", gently -weights early PCs keeping intermediate ones play.

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
code: library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(ggplot2) library(wUMAP) library(patchwork)

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: use classic PBMC 3k dataset SeuratData, includes PCA, cell-type annotations, standard clustering already applied.
code: data(pbmc3k.final) pbmc <- UpdateSeuratObject(pbmc3k.final) Idents(pbmc) <- "seurat_annotations"

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: RunWeightedUMAP() drop-replacement RunUMAP(). Setting weight.= "none" reproduces standard result; default weight.= "stdev" scales PC normalised standard deviation. Left: standard UMAP. Right: stdev-weighted UMAP.
code: set.seed(42) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "umap.std",     verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50,                         reduction.name = "umap.wt",      verbose = FALSE) p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") + NoLegend() p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (stdev)") + NoLegend() p1 | p2

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: Three schemes available via weight.: optional mp.filter = TRUE flag applies Marchenko–Pastur (MP) law random matrix theory determine PCs carry genuine signal. Given nn cells pp features, theoretical noise ceiling λmax=(1+pn)2\lambda_{\max} = \left(1 + \sqrt{\frac{p}{n}}\right)^2 PCs whose variance σi2≤λmax\sigma_i^2 \leq \lambda_{\max} zeroed base weights computed, remaining weights renormalised. works weight.scheme. stdev (default) vs prop.var weighting.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "umap.wt.pv", verbose = FALSE) p3 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt.pv", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var)") + NoLegend() p2 | p3

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: mp.filter = TRUE uses random matrix theory identify PCs carry genuine signal noise floor predicted random matrix dimensions. PCs receive non-zero weight; remainder zeroed regardless base weighting scheme. can inspect many PCs retained (non-zero weight):  stdev weighting vs stdev + MP filtering.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, mp.filter = TRUE,                         reduction.name = "umap.mp", verbose = FALSE) mp_weights <- Misc(pbmc[["umap.mp"]])$weights cat(sprintf("%d / %d PCs above noise floor (non-zero weight)\n",             sum(mp_weights > 0), length(mp_weights))) #> 8 / 50 PCs above noise floor (non-zero weight) barplot(mp_weights, names.arg = seq_along(mp_weights),         xlab = "PC", ylab = "stdev weight (after MP filtering)",         main = "Weights after Marchenko-Pastur filtering (zero = noise)",         col = ifelse(mp_weights > 0, "#4393C3", "#D1D1D1"),         border = NA, las = 1) p_mp <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.mp", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (stdev + mp.filter)") + NoLegend() p2 | p_mp

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: weight.factor (0–1) continuously blends unweighted (0) fully weighted (1) embedding: weight.factor controls blend standard fully weighted.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "stdev",                         weight.factor = 0.25, reduction.name = "umap.wf25",                         verbose = FALSE) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "stdev",                         weight.factor = 0.75, reduction.name = "umap.wf75",                         verbose = FALSE) pa <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0") + NoLegend() pb <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wf25", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0.25") + NoLegend() pc <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wf75", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 0.75") + NoLegend() pd <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("weight.factor = 1") + NoLegend() (pa | pb) / (pc | pd)

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: datasets PC 1 explains dramatically variance others, log.scale = TRUE first applies log1p() weights normalising. compresses dynamic range intermediate PCs still contribute layout. Standard (left), stdev weighted (centre), log-scaled stdev weights (right).
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:50, weight.by = "stdev",                         log.scale = TRUE, reduction.name = "umap.log",                         verbose = FALSE) DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("Standard") + NoLegend() | DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.wt",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("stdev") + NoLegend() | DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.log", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) + ggtitle("stdev + log.scale") + NoLegend()

what: Consistent clustering and UMAP with RunWeightedNeighbors()
title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: common pitfall: clustering one KNN graph visualising another. RunWeightedNeighbors() stores weighted embedding builds KNN SNN graphs one step, clustering UMAP share exactly nearest-neighbour structure. Clustering UMAP derived weighted KNN graph.
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:50, prefix = "wt", verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn", resolution = 0.5,                      verbose = FALSE)  pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", reduction.name = "umap.consistent",                         verbose = FALSE) DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.consistent", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Consistent weighted clustering + UMAP") + NoLegend()

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: RunLocalPCAUMAP() principled alternative global variance weighting. Rather rescaling PC axes globally, measures distance every pair neighbours locally-fitted PCA basis — capturing predominant direction variation cell’s neighbourhood (e.g. tangent trajectory) de-emphasising transverse noise. local.weight.parameter (default "stdev") additionally weights local PC direction contribution local variance, analogous weight.weights global PCs. Algorithm: Find global kk nearest neighbours every cell PCA space (RANN). cell ii, centre kk-neighbourhood compute compact SVD. Optionally weight local.dims principal directions local variance contribution (local.weight.). Re-express displacement vectors neighbours weighted local basis. Report Euclidean norm refined distance pass n×kn \times k distance matrix uwot::umap. consistent clustering UMAP topology, use RunLocalPCANeighbors() build KNN/SNN graphs first, RunLocalPCAUMAP() matching parameters. Standard UMAP (left) vs local PCA UMAP stdev weighting (right). local PCA approach can reveal trajectory-like structure compressed standard UMAP distance metric insensitive predominant local direction variation.
code: set.seed(42) # Build local PCA KNN/SNN graphs (stdev weighting — default) pbmc <- RunLocalPCANeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                              prefix = "lp", verbose = FALSE)  # Cluster on the local PCA SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "lp_snn", resolution = 0.5,                      verbose = FALSE) # UMAP with matching local PCA distances pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap", verbose = FALSE) p_std <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std",  label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") + NoLegend() p_lp  <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap",   label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Local PCA UMAP (stdev)") + NoLegend() p_std | p_lp

title: Variance-Weighted UMAP with wUMAP
text: quantitative evaluation weighting schemes (including "mp" RunLocalPCAUMAP) independent protein-based ground truth (cbmc CITE-seq), see Benchmark article.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lachlan Dryburgh. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dryburgh L (2026). wUMAP: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP Seurat Objects. R package version 0.1.0.
code: @Manual{,   title = {wUMAP: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects},   author = {Lachlan Dryburgh},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

what: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: wUMAP provides RunWeightedUMAP(), drop-replacement Seurat’s RunUMAP() scales principal component weight derived variance explained running manifold learning. gives biologically informative PCs influence final embedding.

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: Standard UMAP treats every PC dimension equally computing cell–cell distances. practice, PC1 might explain 15% variance PC30 explains 0.3% — yet contribute equally distance matrix. Weighting PCs variance explained amplifies axes real biological variation suppresses noise-dominated dimensions.

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: Dependencies (uwot, SeuratObject) installed automatically.
code: # Install from GitHub (requires remotes) remotes::install_github("LachlanD/weightedUMAP")

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
code: library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already computed (e.g. via RunPCA()) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var") DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: weight.factor (0–1) blends standard UMAP (0) fully weighted UMAP (1):
code: # Standard UMAP — all PCs equal pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0)  # Half-strength weighting pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 0.5)  # Full weighting (default) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         weight.factor = 1)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
code: pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var")    # default pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "stdev") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "none")

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: log.scale = TRUE, log1p() applied weights re-normalised embedding. compresses gap high- low-variance PCs intermediate PCs receive relatively influence, still preserving rank ordering weights.
code: # Full weighting, no log scaling (default) pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = FALSE)  # Log-scaled weights — intermediate PCs get relatively more influence pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         log.scale = TRUE)

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: key difference PC scores scaled distances computed: Standard UMAP (weight.= "none") — every PC contributes equally, noisy high-index PCs can blur cluster boundaries. Weighted UMAP (weight.= "prop.var") — PCs multiplied weight UMAP runs, dominant axes biological variation drive layout.
code: library(patchwork)  # Run both on the same object pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "umap.std") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap")  # Compare side by side p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap.std", label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard UMAP") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap",  label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Weighted UMAP (prop.var)") p1 | p2

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: returned object gains new DimReduc reduction.name. misc slot stores chosen weight., weights vector, source.reduction, dims.used reproducibility.
code: RunWeightedUMAP(   object,   reduction      = "pca",       # source reduction   dims           = NULL,        # e.g. 1:30; NULL uses all available dims   weight.by      = "prop.var",  # weighting scheme (see table above)   weight.factor  = 1,           # 0 = standard UMAP, 1 = fully weighted   log.scale      = FALSE,       # compress weight dynamic range with log1p()   graph          = NULL,        # name of KNN graph from RunWeightedNeighbors()                                 #   e.g. "wt_nn"; overrides dims/weight.by/reduction   reduction.name = "wt.umap",   # name stored in the Seurat object   reduction.key  = "wtUMAP_",   # column prefix: wtUMAP_1, wtUMAP_2, ...   n.neighbors    = 30L,   n.components   = 2L,   metric         = "euclidean",   min.dist       = 0.3,   spread         = 1,   seed.use       = 42L,   verbose        = TRUE,   ...                           # passed to uwot::umap() )

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: default, FindNeighbors() RunUMAP() build neighbour graph — unweighted PCA scores — topology used clustering one used UMAP embedding can differ. RunWeightedNeighbors() computes weighted PC embeddings , stores "<prefix>.pca" reduction, builds KNN/SNN graphs weighted space. call RunWeightedUMAP(graph = "wt_nn", ...), looks stored weighted embedding runs UMAP directly — clustering visualisation operate weighted PC space k. Note: graph argument tells RunWeightedUMAP weighted space use (convention "wt_nn" → "wt.pca"). actual UMAP computed embedding, binary adjacency values graph. Compare standard (inconsistent) workflow:
code: library(Seurat) library(wUMAP) library(patchwork)  # Step 1 — build weighted KNN/SNN graphs (k = 20 by default) #           stores 'wt.pca' embedding and 'wt_nn' / 'wt_snn' graphs pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, weight.by = "prop.var",                               k.param = 20, prefix = "wt")  # Step 2 — cluster on the weighted SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn")  # Step 3 — UMAP in the same weighted space #           set n.neighbors to match the k.param used above pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", n.neighbors = 20,                          reduction.name = "wt.umap")  DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE) # Standard workflow — neighbour graphs computed independently pbmc <- FindNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30)           # unweighted, k = 20 pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc) pbmc <- RunUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30)                 # separate NN computation

title: Variance-Weighted PCA UMAP for Seurat Objects
text: MIT

what: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
text: Computes per-cell local PCA distances (see RunLocalPCAUMAP) stores resulting KNN SNN graphs Seurat object.  graphs can passed FindClusters (via graph.name argument) RunLocalPCAUMAP (via nn_method argument ...), ensuring clustering visualisation share exactly local-PCA nearest-neighbour structure.

title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
code: RunLocalPCANeighbors(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   k.param = 20L,   local.dims = NULL,   local.weight.by = c("stdev", "prop.var", "none"),   mp.filter = FALSE,   prune.SNN = 1/15,   prefix = "lp",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
text: object Seurat object PCA already run. reduction Name PCA reduction use. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector PC dimensions use. Default: available. k.param Number nearest neighbours. Default: 20L. local.dims Number local PCA directions retain computing distances.  Must \(\leq k.param\). Default: NULL (uses k.param directions). local.weight.Weighting scheme local PC directions.  See RunLocalPCAUMAP full descriptions.  One "stdev" (default), "prop.var", "none". mp.filter Logical. TRUE, local PC directions per-neighbourhood Marchenko-Pastur noise threshold zeroed base weights computed.  Can combined local.weight.scheme.  Default: FALSE. prune.SNN Jaccard similarity threshold edges removed SNN graph. Default: 1/15. prefix Prefix two stored graphs: <prefix>_nn (KNN, binary) <prefix>_snn (SNN, Jaccard-weighted).  Pass <prefix>_snn FindClusters(graph.name = ...). Default: "lp". verbose Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
text: input Seurat object two new Graph objects: <prefix>_nn — binary KNN graph (symmetric). <prefix>_snn — Jaccard-weighted SNN graph (sparse, pruned).

title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
text: SNN graph sparse matrix Jaccard similarities, pruned prune.SNN (default \(1/15\)), matching Seurat's convention.

title: Build a Local-PCA Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunLocalPCANeighbors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already run  # 1. Build local PCA KNN/SNN graphs — stdev weighting (default, recommended) pbmc <- RunLocalPCANeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 20,                              prefix = "lp")  # 2. Cluster on the local PCA SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "lp_snn", resolution = 0.5)  # 3. Visualise with matching local PCA UMAP (same k, same weighting) pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 20,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap")  DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap", label = TRUE)  # With MP filtering: discard local noise directions, then weight by stdev pbmc <- RunLocalPCANeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 20,                              mp.filter = TRUE, prefix = "lp.mp") pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "lp.mp_snn", resolution = 0.5) pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 20,                         mp.filter = TRUE, reduction.name = "lp.umap.mp") } # }

what: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: Computes UMAP using local PCA metric: cell, distance \(k\) nearest neighbours re-measured locally-fitted PCA basis built neighbourhood.  captures anisotropic local structure (e.g. tangent direction trajectory) de-emphasises transverse noise, producing richer distance measure single global Euclidean metric.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
code: RunLocalPCAUMAP(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   k.param = 30L,   local.dims = NULL,   local.weight.by = c("stdev", "prop.var", "none"),   mp.filter = FALSE,   reduction.name = "lp.umap",   reduction.key = "lpUMAP_",   n.components = 2L,   min.dist = 0.3,   spread = 1,   seed.use = 42L,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: object Seurat object PCA already run. reduction Name PCA reduction use. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector PC dimensions use. Default: available. k.param Number nearest neighbours local PCA. Default: 30L. local.dims Number local PCA directions retain computing refined distances.  Must \(\leq\) k.param. Default: NULL (uses k.param directions). local.weight.Weighting scheme applied local PC directions. Analogous weight.RunWeightedUMAP, applied local singular values neighbourhood SVD. "stdev" Weight local standard deviation (\(s_l / \sum s\)).  Default.  Gently emphasises dominant local direction without collapsing minor variation. "prop.var" Weight local PC proportion local variance (\(s_l^2 / \sum s^2\)); aggressively emphasises leading local direction. "none" weighting; equal contribution local PCs. mp.filter Logical. TRUE, local PC directions whose local variance per-neighbourhood Marchenko-Pastur noise threshold (\(\lambda_{\max} = (1 + \sqrt{d/k})^2\)) zeroed local.weight.weights computed, remaining weights renormalised.  Can combined local.weight.scheme.  Default: FALSE. reduction.name Name store UMAP result. Default: "lp.umap". reduction.key Column-name prefix UMAP dimensions. Default: "lpUMAP_". n.components Number UMAP dimensions. Default: 2L. min.dist UMAP min_dist parameter. Default: 0.3. spread UMAP spread parameter. Default: 1. seed.use Random seed. Default: 42L. verbose Print progress messages. Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded umap.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: input Seurat object new DimReduc stored reduction.name.  misc slot records source.reduction, dims.used, k.param, local.dims, local.weight..

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: Optionally, local PC direction can weighted local variance contribution (local.weight.), analogous weight.weights global PCs RunWeightedUMAP.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
text: Find global k.param nearest neighbours every cell supplied PCA embedding (using RANN). cell \(\), collect \(k\)-neighbourhood, centre neighbourhood mean, compute compact SVD. Optionally weight local.dims local PC directions local variance contribution (local.weight.). Report (weighted) Euclidean norm local-PC space refined distance. Pass \(n \times k\) index/distance matrices directly umap precomputed k-NN graph.

title: UMAP with Local PCA Distances — RunLocalPCAUMAP
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # Assumes pbmc has PCA already run  # Local PCA UMAP with stdev weighting of local PC directions (default) pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap")  # Unweighted local PCA for comparison pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         local.weight.by = "none",                         reduction.name = "lp.umap.unweighted")  # With MP filtering: zero local noise directions, then apply stdev weights pbmc <- RunLocalPCAUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30, k.param = 30,                         mp.filter = TRUE,                         reduction.name = "lp.umap.mp")  # Compare standard, unweighted local PCA, and weighted local PCA p1 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap",               label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Standard") p2 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap.unweighted", label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Local PCA (unweighted)") p3 <- DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "lp.umap",            label = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Local PCA (stdev)") p1 | p2 | p3 } # }

what: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: Scales principal component variance-derived weight (weighting RunWeightedUMAP), stores result new DimReduc, builds KNN/SNN graph via Seurat's FindNeighbors().  resulting graphs can passed directly FindClusters , via graph argument RunWeightedUMAP, UMAP — ensuring clustering visualisation share exactly nearest-neighbour structure.

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
code: RunWeightedNeighbors(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   weight.by = c("stdev", "prop.var", "none"),   k.param = 20L,   reduction.name = "wt.pca",   reduction.key = "wtPCA_",   prefix = "wt",   weight.factor = 1,   log.scale = FALSE,   mp.filter = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. reduction Name dimensional reduction use input. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector dimensions use. NULL (default), available dimensions used warning raised 50. weight.Weighting scheme applied PC scores UMAP. One : "stdev" Standard deviation, normalised (sdev / sum(sdev)). Default. Gently -weights early PCs keeping intermediate ones play, giving good balance signal emphasis layout completeness. "prop.var" Proportion variance explained (sdev^2 / sum(sdev^2)); aggressively -weights dominant PCs.  Use weight.factor < 1 log.scale = TRUE avoid -compression datasets PC 1 explains large fraction variance. "none" weighting; equivalent standard UMAP PCA scores. k.param Number nearest neighbours KNN graph. Default: 20L. reduction.name Name store intermediate weighted-PC embedding object.  Default: "wt.pca". reduction.key Column-name prefix weighted-PC dimensions. Default: "wtPCA_". prefix Prefix two graphs stored Seurat object: <prefix>_nn (KNN, used UMAP) <prefix>_snn (SNN, used clustering). Pass prefix FindClusters(graph.name = "<prefix>_snn") RunWeightedUMAP(graph = "<prefix>_nn"). Default: "wt". weight.factor number [0, 1] controlling influence weighting scheme.  1 (default) full variance-derived weights applied.  0 PCs contribute equally (standard nearest-neighbour graph).  Must match weight.factor used subsequent RunWeightedUMAP call keep clustering visualisation space. log.scale Logical. TRUE, log1p() applied weights weight.factor interpolation.  match value used subsequent RunWeightedUMAP call. Default: FALSE. mp.filter Logical. TRUE, PCs whose variance Marchenko-Pastur bulk-noise threshold (\(\lambda_{\max} = (1 + \sqrt{p/n})^2\)) zeroed chosen weight.weights computed, remaining weights renormalised.  makes MP orthogonal filtering step compatible weighting scheme.  Ignored weight.= "none" PCs threshold.  Default: FALSE. verbose Print progress messages per-PC weights? Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded FindNeighbors.

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
text: input Seurat object : new DimReduc stored reduction.name containing variance-weighted PC embeddings. Two neighbour graphs (<prefix>_nn <prefix>_snn) suitable downstream FindClusters() RunWeightedUMAP(graph = ...).

title: Build a Variance-Weighted Nearest-Neighbour Graph — RunWeightedNeighbors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) library(wUMAP)  # 1. Compute weighted KNN/SNN graphs — stdev weighting (default, recommended) pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, prefix = "wt")  # 2. Cluster on the weighted SNN graph pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt_snn")  # 3. UMAP from the same weighted KNN graph — topology identical to step 2 pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt_nn", n.neighbors = 20,                         reduction.name = "wt.umap")  DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "wt.umap", label = TRUE)  # With MP filtering: discard noise PCs, then apply stdev weights pbmc <- RunWeightedNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30, mp.filter = TRUE,                              prefix = "wt.mp") pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, graph.name = "wt.mp_snn") pbmc <- RunWeightedUMAP(pbmc, graph = "wt.mp_nn",                         reduction.name = "wt.umap.mp") } # }

what: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: Scales principal component embedding weight derived variance explained component, runs UMAP via uwot. result stored new DimReduc returned Seurat object.

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
code: RunWeightedUMAP(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   weight.by = c("stdev", "prop.var", "none"),   graph = NULL,   reduction.name = "wt.umap",   reduction.key = "wtUMAP_",   n.neighbors = 30L,   n.components = 2L,   metric = "euclidean",   min.dist = 0.3,   spread = 1,   seed.use = 42L,   weight.factor = 1,   log.scale = FALSE,   mp.filter = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. reduction Name dimensional reduction use input. Default: "pca". dims Integer vector dimensions use. NULL (default), available dimensions used warning raised 50. weight.Weighting scheme applied PC scores UMAP. One : "stdev" Standard deviation, normalised (sdev / sum(sdev)). Default. Gently -weights early PCs keeping intermediate ones play, giving good balance signal emphasis layout completeness. "prop.var" Proportion variance explained (sdev^2 / sum(sdev^2)); aggressively -weights dominant PCs.  Use weight.factor < 1 log.scale = TRUE avoid -compression datasets PC 1 explains large fraction variance. "none" weighting; equivalent standard UMAP PCA scores. graph Optional name precomputed Seurat KNN graph produced RunWeightedNeighbors() (e.g. "wt_nn").  supplied, UMAP run weighted PC embedding generated graph (stored RunWeightedNeighbors() "<prefix>.pca", <prefix> derived stripping _nn suffix graph name).  ensures UMAP topology computed exactly weighted space clustering graphs.  dims, weight., reduction, metric arguments ignored graph supplied.  Set n.neighbors match k.param used RunWeightedNeighbors().  Default: NULL. reduction.name Name store new UMAP reduction object. Default: "wt.umap". reduction.key Column‐name prefix UMAP dimensions, e.g. "wtUMAP_" produces column names wtUMAP_1, wtUMAP_2, … n.neighbors Number nearest neighbours used UMAP. Default: 30. n.components Number UMAP dimensions compute. Default: 2. metric Distance metric passed uwot::umap(). Default: "euclidean". min.dist Effective minimum distance embedded points. Default: 0.3. spread Effective scale embedded points. Default: 1. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Set NULL skip set.seed(). Default: 42. weight.factor number [0, 1] scales influence chosen weighting scheme.  1 (default) full variance-derived weights applied.  0 PCs contribute equally, equivalent weight.= "none" (standard UMAP).  Intermediate values blend uniformly-weighted fully-weighted embeddings. log.scale Logical. TRUE, log1p() applied weights weight.factor interpolation, compressing dynamic range intermediate PCs receive relatively influence.  Ignored weight.= "none".  Default: FALSE. mp.filter Logical. TRUE, PCs whose variance Marchenko-Pastur bulk-noise threshold (\(\lambda_{\max} = (1 + \sqrt{p/n})^2\)) zeroed chosen weight.weights computed, remaining weights renormalised.  makes MP orthogonal filtering step compatible weighting scheme.  Ignored weight.= "none" PCs threshold.  Default: FALSE. verbose Print progress messages per-PC weights? Default: TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded uwot::umap().

title: Run Variance-Weighted UMAP on a Seurat Object — RunWeightedUMAP
text: input Seurat object new DimReduc named reduction.name appended. misc slot reduction stores: weight.— chosen weighting scheme weights    — numeric vector weights (one per dimension used) source.reduction — name source reduction dims.used — integer vector dimensions used
